classes.name: Alarm1
classes.label: Alarm
classes.comment:
An alarm is a device that signals the existence of a condition or situation that is outside the boundaries of normal expectation or that activates such a device.

Alarms include the provision of break glass buttons and manual pull boxes that are used to activate alarms.

classes.name: Alarm2
classes.label: Alarm
classes.comment: A warning of danger or potential undesired situation.

classes.name: Audio visual appliance1
classes.label: Audio visual appliance
classes.comment:
An audio-visual appliance is a device that displays, captures, transmits, or receives audio or video.

Audio-visual appliances may be fixed in place or may be able to be moved from one space to another. They may require an electrical supply that may be supplied either by an electrical circuit or provided from a local battery source. Audio-visual appliances may be connected to data circuits including specialist circuits for audio visual purposes only.

classes.name: Boiler1
classes.label: Boiler
classes.comment: A boiler is a closed, pressure-rated vessel in which water or other fluid is heated using an energy source such as natural gas, heating oil, or electricity. The fluid in the vessel is then circulated out of the boiler for use in various processes or heating applications.

classes.name: Building1
classes.label: Building
classes.comment: A building represents a structure that provides shelter for its occupants or contents and stands in one place. The building is also used to provide a basic element within the spatial structure hierarchy for the components of a building project (together with site, storey, and space).

classes.name: Building2
classes.label: Building
classes.comment: A category that includes devices as described by building related data models, such as IFC and FIEMSER

classes.name: Burner1
classes.label: Burner
classes.comment: A burner is a device that converts fuel into heat through combustion. It includes gas, oil, and wood burners.

classes.name: Chiller1
classes.label: Chiller
classes.comment: A chiller is a device used to remove heat from a liquid via a vapor-compression or absorption refrigeration cycle to cool a fluid, typically water or a mixture of water and glycol. The chilled fluid is then used to cool and dehumidify air in a building.

classes.name: Coil1
classes.label: Coil
classes.comment:
A coil is a device used to provide heat transfer between non-mixing media. A common example is a cooling coil, which utilizes a finned coil in which circulates chilled water, antifreeze, or refrigerant that is used to remove heat from air moving across the surface of the coil. A coil may be used either for heating or cooling purposes by placing a series of tubes (the coil) carrying a heating or cooling fluid into an airstream. The coil may be constructed from tubes bundled in a serpentine form or from finned tubes that give a extended heat transfer surface.

Coils may also be used for non-airflow cases such as embedded in a floor slab.

classes.name: Compressor1
classes.label: Compressor
classes.comment: A compressor is a device that compresses a fluid typically used in a refrigeration circuit.

classes.name: Controller1
classes.label: Controller
classes.comment:
A controller is a device that monitors inputs and controls outputs within a building automation system.

A controller may be physical (having placement within a spatial structure) or logical (a software interface or aggregated within a programmable physical controller).

classes.name: Current1
classes.label: Current
classes.comment: A property related to measurements measured in Ampere (A).

classes.name: Current2
classes.label: Current
classes.comment: The movement of electricity in a particular direction.

classes.name: Damper1
classes.label: Damper
classes.comment: A damper typically participates in an HVAC duct distribution system and is used to control or modulate the flow of air.

classes.name: Filter1
classes.label: Filter
classes.comment: A filter is an apparatus used to remove particulate or gaseous matter from fluids and gases.

classes.name: Flow controller1
classes.label: Flow controller
classes.comment: The distribution flow element FlowController defines the occurrence of elements of a distribution system that are used to regulate flow through a distribution system. Examples include dampers, valves, switches, and relays.

classes.name: Flow meter1
classes.label: Flow meter
classes.comment: A flow meter is a device that is used to measure the flow rate in a system.

classes.name: Flow moving device1
classes.label: Flow moving device
classes.comment: The distribution flow element FlowMovingDevice defines the occurrence of an apparatus used to distribute, circulate or perform conveyance of fluids, including liquids and gases (such as a pump or fan), and typically participates in a flow distribution system.

classes.name: Flow storage device1
classes.label: Flow storage device
classes.comment: The distribution flow element FlowStorageDevice defines the occurrence of a device that participates in a distribution system and is used for temporary storage (such as a tank).

classes.name: Flow terminal1
classes.label: Flow terminal
classes.comment: The distribution flow element FlowTerminal defines the occurrence of a permanently attached element that acts as a terminus or beginning of a distribution system (such as an air outlet, drain, water closet, or sink). A terminal is typically a point at which a system interfaces with an external environment.

classes.name: Humidifier1
classes.label: Humidifier
classes.comment: A humidifier is a device that adds moisture into the air.

classes.name: Humidity1
classes.label: Humidity
classes.comment: A saref:Property related to some measurements that are characterized by a certain value that is measured in a humidity unit

classes.name: Humidity2
classes.label: Humidity
classes.comment: Outdoor humidity

classes.name: Interceptor1
classes.label: Interceptor
classes.comment: An interceptor is a device designed and installed in order to separate and retain deleterious, hazardous or undesirable matter while permitting normal sewage or liquids to discharge into a collection system by gravity.

classes.name: Lamp1
classes.label: Lamp
classes.comment: A lamp is an artificial light source such as a light bulb or tube.

classes.name: Occupancy1
classes.label: Occupancy
classes.comment: Usage of a space.

classes.name: Occupancy2
classes.label: Occupancy
classes.comment: A saref:Property related to some measurements that are characterized by a certain value (saref:hasValue property) that is measured in a unit of measure for occupancy

classes.name: Power Consumption1
classes.label: Power Consumption
classes.comment: The rate, per unit time, at which energy is consumed.

classes.name: Power Consumption2
classes.label: Power Consumption
classes.comment: The amount of power consumed.

classes.name: Room1
classes.label: Room
classes.comment: A room in a building. This class may be used to group devices.

classes.name: Room2
classes.label: Room
classes.comment: A room in a building space enclosed by surfaces, this could also be modelled as role of space, not subclass of the space itself.

classes.name: Sensor1
classes.label: Sensor
classes.comment: Device, agent (including humans), or software (simulation) involved in, or implementing, a Procedure. Sensors respond to a stimulus, e.g., a change in the environment, or input data composed from the results of prior Observations, and generate a Result. Sensors can be hosted by Platforms.

classes.name: Sensor2
classes.label: Sensor
classes.comment:
A device that detects and responds to events or changes in the physical environment such as light, motion, or temperature changes.
A device that has category saref:Sensor and performs a saref:SensingFunction.

classes.name: Sensor3
classes.label: Sensor
classes.comment: A device that detects and responds to events or changes in the physical environment such as light, motion, or temperature changes. Further, a device of category saref:Sensor that performs a saref:SensingFunction.

classes.name: Space heater1
classes.label: Space heater
classes.comment:
Space heaters utilize a combination of radiation and/or natural convection using a heating source such as electricity, steam or hot water to heat a limited space or area. Examples of space heaters include radiators, convectors, baseboard and finned-tube heaters.

UnitaryEquipment should be used for packaged units supporting a combination of heating, cooling, and/or dehumidification; Coil should be used for coil-based floor heating.

classes.name: Switching device1
classes.label: Switching device
classes.comment:
A switch is used in a cable distribution system (electrical circuit) to control or modulate the flow of electricity.

Switches include those used for electrical power, communications, audio-visual, or other distribution system types as determined by the available ports.

classes.name: Tank1
classes.label: Tank
classes.comment: A tank is a vessel or container in which a fluid or gas is stored for later use.

classes.name: Temperature1
classes.label: Temperature
classes.comment: A saref:Property related to some measurements that are characterized by a certain value that is measured in a temperature unit (degree_Celsius, degree_Fahrenheit, or degree_kelvin)

classes.name: Temperature2
classes.label: Temperature
classes.comment: Outdoor temperature

classes.name: Transformer1
classes.label: Transformer
classes.comment:
A transformer is an inductive stationary device that transfers electrical energy from one circuit to another.

Transformer is used to transform electric power; conversion of electric signals for other purposes is handled at other entities: Controller converts arbitrary signals, AudioVisualAppliance converts signals for audio or video streams, and CommunicationsAppliance converts signals for data or other communications usage.

classes.name: Tube bundle1
classes.label: Tube bundle
classes.comment: A tube bundle is a device consisting of tubes and bundles of tubes used for heat transfer and contained typically within other energy conversion devices, such as a chiller or coil.

classes.name: Voltage1
classes.label: Voltage
classes.comment: Voltage in Volts

classes.name: Voltage2
classes.label: Voltage
classes.comment: The force of an electric current, measured in volts.

properties.name: active power2
properties.label: active power
properties.comment: Active power consumed per phase. The unit code (text) is given using the UN/CEFACT Common Codes .

properties.name: address1
properties.label: address
properties.comment: To describe the address of a Location

properties.name: address2
properties.label: address
properties.comment: The mailing address .

properties.name: description1
properties.label: description
properties.comment: A description of this item

properties.name: flow resistance range1
properties.label: flow resistance range
properties.comment: Allowable range of frictional resistance against which the fluid is being pumped. Usually measured in Pascals (Pa, N/m2).

properties.name: flow resistance range2
properties.label: flow resistance range
properties.comment: Allowable range of frictional resistance against which the fluid is being pumped. Usually measured in Pascals (Pa, N/m2).

properties.name: fluid flow rate range1
properties.label: fluid flow rate range
properties.comment: Possible range of fluid flowrate that can be delivered. Usually measured in m3/s.

properties.name: fluid flow rate range2
properties.label: fluid flow rate range
properties.comment: Possible range of fluid flowrate that can be delivered. Usually measured in m3/s.

properties.name: location2
properties.label: location
properties.comment: Geojson reference to the item. It can be Point, LineString, Polygon, MultiPoint, MultiLineString or MultiPolygon

properties.name: name2
properties.label: name
properties.comment: The name of this item

properties.name: nominal efficiency2
properties.label: nominal efficiency
properties.comment: Nominal chiller efficiency under nominal conditions.

properties.name: nominal supply voltage offset1
properties.label: nominal supply voltage offset
properties.comment: The maximum and minimum allowed voltage of the supply e.g. boundaries of 380V/440V may be applied for a nominal voltage of 400V.

properties.name: nominal supply voltage offset2
properties.label: nominal supply voltage offset
properties.comment: The maximum and minimum allowed voltage of the supply e.g. boundaries of 380V/440V may be applied for a nominal voltage of 400V.

properties.name: operation temperature range1
properties.label: operation temperature range
properties.comment: Allowable operation ambient (air, fluid) temperature range. Usually measured in degrees Kelvin (K).

properties.name: operation temperature range2
properties.label: operation temperature range
properties.comment: Allowable operation ambient (air, fluid) temperature range. Usually measured in degrees Kelvin (K).

properties.name: outlet temperature range1
properties.label: outlet temperature range
properties.comment: Allowable outlet temperature of either the water or the steam. Usually measured in degrees Kelvin (K).

properties.name: outlet temperature range2
properties.label: outlet temperature range
properties.comment: Allowable outlet temperature of either the water or the steam. Usually measured in degrees Kelvin (K).

properties.name: primary air flow rate range1
properties.label: primary air flow rate range
properties.comment: Maximum primary airflow that can be delivered. Usually measured in m3/s.

properties.name: primary air flow rate range2
properties.label: primary air flow rate range
properties.comment: Minimum primary airflow that can be delivered. Usually measured in m3/s.

properties.name: primary current1
properties.label: primary current
properties.comment: The current that is going to be transformed and that runs into the transformer on the primary side. Usually measured in Ampere (A).

properties.name: primary voltage1
properties.label: primary voltage
properties.comment: The voltage that is going to be transformed and that runs into the transformer on the primary side. Usually measured in Volts (V, W/A).

properties.name: pump flow rate range1
properties.label: pump flow rate range
properties.comment: Allowable range of volume of fluid being pumped against the resistance specified. Usually measured in kg/s.

properties.name: pump flow rate range2
properties.label: pump flow rate range
properties.comment: Allowable range of volume of fluid being pumped against the resistance specified. Usually measured in kg/s.

properties.name: reactive power2
properties.label: reactive power
properties.comment: Fundamental frequency reactive power. The unit code (text) is given using the UN/CEFACT Common Codes

properties.name: secondary air flow rate range1
properties.label: secondary air flow rate range
properties.comment: Maximum secondary airflow that can be delivered. Usually measured in Pascals (Pa, N/m2).

properties.name: secondary air flow rate range2
properties.label: secondary air flow rate range
properties.comment: Maximum secondary airflow that can be delivered. Usually measured in Pascals (Pa, N/m2).

properties.name: secondary current1
properties.label: secondary current
properties.comment: The current that has been transformed and is running out of the transformer on the secondary side. Usually measured in Ampere (A).

properties.name: secondary voltage1
properties.label: secondary voltage
properties.comment: The voltage that has been transformed and is running out of the transformer on the secondary side. Usually measured in Volts (V, W/A).

properties.name: source2
properties.label: source
properties.comment: A sequence of characters giving the original source of the entity data as a URL. Recommended to be the fully qualified domain name of the source provider, or the URL to the source object

properties.name: water inlet temperature range1
properties.label: water inlet temperature range
properties.comment: Allowable water inlet temperature range. Usually measured in degrees Kelvin (K).

properties.name: water inlet temperature range2
properties.label: water inlet temperature range
properties.comment: Allowable water inlet temperature range. Usually measured in degrees Kelvin (K).

properties.name: water pressure range1
properties.label: water pressure range
properties.comment: Allowable water circuit working pressure range. Usually measured in Pascals (Pa, N/m2).

properties.name: water pressure range2
properties.label: water pressure range
properties.comment: Allowable water circuit working pressure range. Usually measured in Pascals (Pa, N/m2).
